referential_task.1.player.chat_transcrip: [21:18:57] director: Basket 1: the animal-shaped wicker basket—looks like a duck/bird with a beak and a little blue eye on the head (head points to the right). Oval body with a lid seam, and a flat handle/strap across the top. Let me know when you’ve found it.  [21:20:29] matcher: I see a rabbit shaped basket and a mouse shaped basket, but neither has blue eyes  [21:20:39] director: Clarifying basket 1: it’s a SIDE-PROFILE duck/bird-shaped wicker basket (not a round animal face). The head sticks out with a beak pointing to the RIGHT, and there’s a flat strap-like handle lying across the top. One small bead-like eye is visible on the head (looks bluish). Do you see that duck profile one?  [21:21:15] matcher: I see one that looks like a rabbit, but it meets the other criteria  [21:21:24] director: Yes—go with that one for basket 1 as long as it’s the only animal-shaped basket in SIDE PROFILE with a protruding head/beak pointing right and a flat strap handle across the top (not the round face-on animal with little feet). Confirm when placed.  [21:22:18] matcher: Done  [21:22:26] director: Basket 2: a round, bucket-like wicker basket with a SINGLE very dark (almost black) thick arched handle. The body is medium-dark brown and there’s a lighter band/rim around the top edge. Please confirm when you’ve got it.  [21:23:23] matcher: Got it  [21:23:26] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style basket with a hinged LID that’s flipped open/up on the right side. It has a tall central vertical handle/strap rising from the middle, and the weave is light tan. Confirm when placed.  [21:23:39] matcher: Done  [21:23:42] director: Basket 4: an oval basket with a high arched handle; the handle has light/white wrapping bands at intervals (and small reddish accents where it meets). The basket body is pale with horizontal striping and a slightly pointed/boat-like oval shape. Confirm when placed.  [21:25:08] matcher: Are there 2 white wrapping bands? One on each side of the handle?  [21:25:11] director: Yes—basket 4’s handle has light/white wraps on both sides (near the upper parts of the handle), plus a couple small reddish wrap spots where the handle joins the rim. The basket is a pale oval “boat” shape.  [21:25:22] matcher: done  [21:25:25] director: Basket 5: a shallow rectangular basket with a chunky, rope-twist style handle arching over it (same brown color). The front has a very regular blocky checker weave, and the basket is wider than it is tall. Confirm when placed.  [21:26:14] matcher: handle and basket are all the same color?  [21:26:21] director: Yes—basket 5 is pretty much all one warm medium-brown: the handle isn’t wrapped in white or a different color. Key tells are the thick *twisted-rope* looking handle and the shallow wide rectangular shape with a neat blocky checker weave on the front.  [21:26:33] matcher: done  [21:26:37] director: Basket 6: the ROUND, lidded animal-face basket—looks like a cat face looking toward you, with little ears, bead eyes, and painted whiskers/nose. It sits on small little feet/pegs. Confirm when placed.  [21:26:48] matcher: done  [21:26:55] director: Basket 7: a rectangular tote with an arched handle, but the key is it’s two-tone—green/teal woven sides with an orange-brown rim/top. There’s also a wavy/scalloped band across the front just under the handle. Confirm when placed.  [21:27:32] matcher: got it  [21:27:35] director: Basket 8: a short oval basket with a dark gray/black handle and dark gray weave, BUT with light/white vertical accent stakes around the sides (like thin pale stripes). The rim is slightly lighter brown. Confirm when placed.  [21:28:27] matcher: got it  [21:28:30] director: Basket 9: tall cylindrical basket (like a laundry hamper) in medium brown with two small loop handles near the top. The sides have vertical slat-like weave panels. Open top (no lid). Confirm when placed.  [21:28:55] matcher: placed  [21:28:58] director: Basket 10: a shallow ROUND tray-style basket with a single pale handle arcing over it. The base has a clear spiral “coil” pattern in the weave (like a bullseye), and the rim is low. Confirm when placed.  [21:29:40] matcher: Is the rim darker than the rest of the basket?  [21:29:42] director: Not really—basket 10 is mostly light tan throughout. The most distinctive feature is the obvious spiral-coiled base weave in the center, with a simple pale handle over a shallow round tray.  [21:30:32] matcher: Is the middle of the bottom of the basket darker in color?  [21:30:35] director: Yes, the very center of the spiral base can look a bit darker/denser, but it’s still within a light tan palette overall. If you see a shallow round basket where the bottom looks like a woven spiral target, that’s basket 10.  [21:30:37] matcher: got  [21:30:40] director: Basket 11: a deep, round open basket with two small upright side handles (like little ear loops). It’s tightly woven in medium brown, and there’s a distinctive darker zigzag/chevron band running around the lower part. Confirm when placed.  [21:30:44] matcher: it  [21:30:48] director: Basket 12: a medium oval basket with a single arched handle, light brown overall. The top rim has two “pinched” triangular corners/points (one on each side), giving it a slightly squared-off oval mouth. The sides have open vertical slats/spacing. Confirm when placed.  [21:31:20] matcher: One second...checking placement  [21:31:23] director: Take your time—basket 12 should look like an oval with two little peaked/pinched corners at the rim (almost like subtle cat-ear points), plus one smooth handle and a fairly open slatted weave on the sides. Let me know if you see a close distractor.  [21:33:42] matcher: basket 11 looks like it has a smaller basket inside?  [21:33:50] director: Clarifying basket 11: it should be ONE deep round tub basket (not nested), with TWO small loop handles sticking up on opposite sides (no big arch handle). The key identifier is a darker zigzag/chevron band wrapping around the lower part. If yours looks like it has a basket inside, that’s probably a distractor—look for the chevron-band one.  [21:35:57] matcher: Okay, I placed the only one with 2 small handles  [21:36:04] director: Basket 12 (final): light-brown oval basket with ONE arched handle, and the rim has two distinct pinched/peaked corners (one on each side). The sides are fairly open/slatted with vertical gaps. Please confirm when you’ve got that one in position 12.  [21:37:23] matcher: Okay, I think we're ready to submit  [21:37:32] director: Great—if you’re submitting now, just double-check basket 12 is the light-brown oval with ONE arch handle and two pinched/peaked corners on the rim (ear-like points) plus open slatted sides (not the white-wrapped handle one, not the shallow rectangular with rope handle, and not the round spiral tray).  [21:38:30] matcher: I'm double-check #10  [21:38:38] director: Basket 10 is the shallow ROUND tray-like one with a single pale arch handle, and the inside/bottom shows a very obvious spiral “bullseye” weave. It’s not oval and not rectangular—low rim, round platter look. Does that match what you picked?  [21:39:37] matcher: No, so I replaced it with the lighter colored shallow basket that has a darker rim  [21:39:40] director: For basket 10, prioritize the *spiral bullseye base* over rim color. In the target, it’s mostly light tan and the key feature is that clear spiral/coil pattern in the center of the bottom, with one pale handle over a shallow *round* tray. If your replacement doesn’t have that spiral base, keep looking for the spiral-bottom round tray.  [21:40:42] matcher: both of the shallower baskets have a spiral base  [21:40:46] director: Then choose the one that’s the flattest/most tray-like and widest, with a low rim and a single simple pale handle; it should look like a round serving tray with a handle, not like a deeper bowl. Basket 10 is the shallowest round one.  [21:41:08] matcher: I did, so I think we're good  [21:41:16] director: Sounds good—final check: basket 12 is the light-brown oval with ONE arched handle and two pinched/peaked corners on the rim (ear-like points) plus open slatted sides. If that’s what you have, go ahead and submit.  [21:41:21] matcher: Thanks for great instructions  [21:41:27] director: Basket 1: the side-profile animal-shaped wicker basket (duck/bird-like) with a head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT, a flat strap handle across the top, and a small bead-like eye on the head. Confirm when placed.

referential_task.1.player.chat_transcrip: [21:11:29] director: Basket 1: the novelty animal-shaped basket—looks like a duck/goose in side profile with a long rounded beak sticking out to the right and a small dark/blue eye; oval coiled-weave body with the opening/handle area on the back/top. Let me know when you’ve got it.  [21:12:27] matcher: Is the handle the ear?  [21:12:35] director: Yes—kind of: there isn’t a tall arch handle. The opening is on the back/top of the animal, and the little curved rim there can look like an “ear.” Key ID: duck/goose profile with a long beak pointing right and one small dark/blue eye (not the cat-face one).  [21:13:17] matcher: got it  [21:13:25] director: Basket 2: a classic rounded pail/bucket basket with a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; medium-brown body with a lighter band around the rim/top. Confirm when placed.  [21:16:25] matcher: Does the basket have a horizontal weave done with a stick type material? or is is a tight vertical weave?  [21:16:28] director: Basket 2 has a tight, fine weave pattern (more like small diagonal/vertical texture), not big “stick” slats. The standout is that thick dark handle and the lighter rim band at the top.  [21:16:54] matcher: got it  [21:16:57] director: Basket 3: rectangular picnic-style basket with a hinged lid flipped open to the right; tall vertical handle rising from the middle; golden wicker, boxy shape with straight sides. Please confirm when you spot it.  [21:18:10] matcher: all set  [21:18:13] director: Basket 4: an oval basket with a tall arch handle wrapped in white bands; the handle has small reddish/burgundy accents near where it meets the basket. The basket body is light tan with some bluish-gray weaving mixed in. Confirm when placed.  [21:19:04] matcher: all set  [21:19:07] director: Basket 5: shallow rectangular basket with a big smooth arch handle; warm orange-brown wicker with a thick braided-looking rim/top edge and an open lattice weave on the sides. Let me know when it’s in position 5.  [21:21:37] matcher: Is the basket a greenish color?  [21:21:45] director: No—basket 5 is **not** greenish. It’s a **warm orange-brown** shallow rectangular basket with a **thick braided/rope-like rim** and **open lattice sides**, plus a smooth rounded arch handle (not the white-wrapped handle).  [21:25:45] matcher: all set  [21:25:48] director: Basket 6: the round lidded “cat face” basket—circular top with a cat’s face (eyes, nose, whiskers) and little ears; pale straw color and it sits on small little feet. Confirm when placed.  [21:26:08] matcher: all set  [21:26:12] director: Basket 7: rectangular basket with a high arch handle; distinctive two-tone body—orange/brown rim and handle, but the main panels are greenish/teal vertical reeds with darker stripes. Confirm when placed.  [21:27:29] matcher: ok  [21:27:32] director: Basket 8: squat oval basket with a single arch handle; dark gray-brown wicker sides with lighter/whitish horizontal bands near the bottom (striped look). More “modern” dark tone compared to most. Confirm when placed.  [21:30:17] matcher: got it i hope  [21:30:25] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical basket (like a hamper) with straight vertical sides and two small loop handles near the rim (one on each side). No big arch handle—more of an open slat/vertical weave in medium-dark brown. Confirm when placed.  [21:30:40] matcher: got it  [21:30:43] director: Basket 10: very shallow round tray-like basket with a single arched handle; the base has a clear spiral coil pattern, and the color is light tan with a reddish-brown section on one side. Confirm when placed.  [21:31:42] matcher: got it  [21:31:45] director: Basket 11: chunky deep round basket with **two** small ear-like side handles (not an arch); thick braided texture, medium brown, with a slightly flared top and dense weave. Confirm when placed.  [21:32:26] matcher: ok  [21:32:28] director: Basket 12: rounded-square/oval basket with a single arch handle; uniform light-to-medium brown, with fairly open vertical slats visible inside and a smooth rim—more “bucket” shape but squarer than basket 2. Confirm when placed.  [21:35:16] matcher: all set  [21:35:24] director: Great—basket 12 is the squarish bucket-style basket with one arch handle, medium-light brown, smooth rim, and open vertical slats visible inside (not the one with the very dark handle, not the shallow lattice rectangle, and not the greenish or dark-gray striped one).

[21:57:58] matcher: confirmed  [21:58:05] director: Basket 2: a classic bucket/pail-shaped wicker basket with a thick, very dark (almost black) single arched handle and a slightly flared rim; dense small weave all around. Please confirm when placed.  [21:58:32] matcher: placed. check it  [21:58:39] director: Yes—basket 2 should be the deep bucket/pail basket with the thick very dark/black arched handle (darkest handle in the set) and a tight uniform weave with a slightly flared rim band. If yours has a lighter handle or an oval/shallow body, it’s a different one.  [21:59:04] matcher: done  [21:59:07] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style basket with a tall central vertical handle and a hinged lid flipped open to the right (you can see the open top). It’s more boxy than the others, with straight sides and a flat top/lid. Confirm when you’ve got it.  [21:59:20] matcher: confirm  [21:59:27] director: Basket 4: an oval/boat-shaped basket with a single arch handle; the body is light tan wicker but has a noticeably darker brown band around the rim/top. The handle also has pale wrapped sections (light bands) on it. Please confirm when placed.  [22:00:51] matcher: confirm  [22:00:54] director: Basket 5: small rectangular tray-like basket with a high arch handle; very “checkerboard” chunky weave on the sides (square blocks), and a thick twisted-rope looking handle. No lid. Confirm when placed.  [22:02:23] matcher: that basket doesnt exist  [22:02:27] director: Let’s double-check basket 5: it’s the squat, rectangular basket with a **very thick braided/twisted handle** forming a big arch, and the **sides look like chunky square/brick weave** (more geometric than the others). If not, tell me what your closest options look like (rectangle vs oval, handle thickness/color), and I’ll refine. [22:04:12] matcher: ok done  [22:04:15] director: Basket 6: the other novelty animal—round, lidded “cat face” basket viewed from front. [22:04:57] matcher: cofnirm  [22:05:00] director: Basket 7: rectangular basket with a wide arch handle and a distinctive two-tone body—green/teal vertical slats or weave on the sides, with a warm orange-brown rim and base trim. Looks like a sturdy tote with squared corners. Confirm when placed.  [22:05:47] matcher: does it have a lid  [22:05:49] director: Basket 7 has **no lid**—it’s an open-top rectangular basket with that green/teal side paneling and orange-brown trim.  [22:06:26] matcher: confirm  [22:06:29] director: Basket 8: small oval basket with a single arch handle; overall dark gray-brown weave with lighter cream/white accent strips near the base and around the body. Looks like a compact purse basket, darker than most. Confirm when placed.  [22:06:44] matcher: confirm  [22:06:47] director: Basket 9: tall cylindrical basket like a wicker barrel/hamper, straight vertical sides, with **two small loop handles** near the top rim (not one big arch). Medium brown, vertical stake-like weave. Confirm when placed.  [22:07:23] matcher: confirm  [22:07:26] director: Basket 10: very shallow, wide round basket/tray with a low single arch handle; the base has a visible spiral coil pattern, and the colors are pale tan with some reddish/pinkish strips woven through. Confirm when placed.  [22:09:00] matcher: what is the differences between basket 4 and basket 10?  [22:09:04] director: Basket 4 vs Basket 10:  - **Basket 4**: deeper **oval/boat-shaped** body (like a small canoe), with a **dark brown rim band** around the top; handle has a couple **light wrapped sections**. It’s not flat. - **Basket 10**: **very shallow round tray** (almost flat) with a **spiral-coiled base** clearly visible; lighter tan with some **reddish/pinkish** weaving; looks like a round platter with a handle.  [22:09:52] matcher: confirm  [22:09:55] director: Basket 11: large open-top round basket with **two integrated side handles** (ear-like loops). It has a distinctive zigzag/chevron weave pattern around the middle and is deeper than basket 10. Medium tan-brown. Confirm when placed.  [22:10:25] matcher: confirm  [22:10:28] director: Basket 12: rounded-square/oval tote basket with a single arch handle; smooth, uniform light brown weave, and a slightly “pinched”/scalloped rim forming subtle points at the front/back. More refined and plain than basket 5. Confirm when placed.  [22:11:12] matcher: does it have a lid  [22:11:14] director: Basket 12 has **no lid**—it’s an open-top tote with one arch handle and that slightly pinched/scalloped rim.  [22:12:07] matcher: confirm  [22:12:14] director: Great—basket 12 is the plain light-brown open tote with ONE arch handle and a subtly pinched/scalloped rim (gentle points front/back), no lid.

If you don’t have any side-profile animal at all, tell me what animal-shaped options you do see. [21:25:05] director: Ok—let’s move to basket 2: a deep, round bucket-style basket with a single thick arched handle that’s VERY dark brown/near-black. [21:27:17] director: For basket 2: it does NOT have light horizontal border stripes at the top/bottom. The key is the thick arched handle that’s very dark brown/near-black, on a deep round bucket-shaped basket with a darker band just under the rim.  [21:29:28] matcher: I spotted one; bt can yo please confirm that the top horizontal border (i.e., "rim") is the same color brown as the basket part and not "darker"?  [21:29:36] director: Yes—on basket 2 the rim/top band reads a bit darker than the main woven body (not the same tone). [21:31:45] matcher: The description sounds like the one I am thinking; however, the entire basket and rim are objectively the same color. The single handle is clearly "near-black."  [21:31:53] director: Basket 3: a light-tan rectangular picnic basket with a tall central handle/strap. The key detail: the lid is open—one top flap is flipped up to the RIGHT, so you can see the basket’s interior. Confirm when you’ve got it.  [21:33:23] matcher: With no basket entered into slot #1, I am unable to input basket #2 into its slot...assuming the basket color discrepancy is subjective. What basket should I put into slot #1?  [21:33:32] director: For slot #1: choose the ONLY animal basket that’s in SIDE PROFILE (facing right), with a pointed beak/muzzle sticking out and just ONE visible blue bead-like eye. [21:34:39] director: Basket 4: a small oval wicker basket with a single handle; the handle is wrapped with light material and has little reddish/pink accents where it attaches. [21:36:19] director: Basket 4 handle attaches in TWO main places (one on each side), not four; look for the light-wrapped handle with small reddish/pink bits near those side attachments.  [21:39:02] matcher: I do not have a basket with two handle connections "with small reddish/pink bits." The basket that meets that criteria has for connections. [21:40:28] matcher: Does the basket contain borders across the top and bottom? If so, please describe their color?  [21:40:31] director: Basket 4: no strong contrasting top/bottom border stripes—overall it’s a light tan weave. [21:42:29] director: Yes—basket 4 has a light tan body, and the handle is even lighter/paler (looks wrapped), with small reddish/pink accents near where it attaches.  [21:43:34] matcher: And the handle orientation is literally left to right, not SE to NW?  [21:43:36] director: Correct—basket 4 handle is a normal upright arch that spans left-to-right over the basket (not a diagonal SE-to-NW handle).  [21:45:23] matcher: The basket body itself shows no contrast in color?  [21:45:25] director: Right—basket 4’s body is basically one light tan color without obvious contrasting bands; the handle being paler is the main contrast.  [21:47:35] matcher: If the basket itself is "narrow," I simply do not have a basket that meets this criteria. [21:53:07] director: Basket 6: the front-facing animal-face basket—round dome/lidded body with TWO small blue bead eyes, a pink/red nose and whisker lines on the woven face (looks like a cat/rabbit head), sitting on little feet. [21:56:14] director: Basket 8: yes—around the top rim there are lighter/whitish accents compared with the darker gray body, and the strongest light banding is near the bottom. [21:56:42] matcher: Done  [21:56:45] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket (like a small hamper) with TWO small loop handles near the top rim (not a big arch). [21:57:56] director: Basket 9 is the more excessively tall, straight-sided cylinder—hamper-like—taller than “average” baskets, with two small loop handles right at the top.  [21:58:27] matcher: Done  [21:58:30] director: Basket 10: a shallow round basket/tray with a single handle; the base has a clear spiral weave pattern, and the handle is a pale smooth arch. [22:04:18] director: Basket 11: a deep, open-top round basket with TWO short ear-like handles (one on each side), no tall arch. [22:06:38] matcher: Basket 10, is the handle wrapped without palpable weaving?  [22:06:40] director: Yes—basket 10 handle looks wrapped/smooth rather than obviously woven/braided.  [22:09:25] matcher: I only had one other two-handled basket. to select. Basket 12, please.  [22:09:29] director: Basket 12: a medium brown oval basket with ONE tall arch handle; the top rim forms a wavy/scalloped “M” shape with two little peaks at the front (like a gentle double-dip). [22:17:29] matcher: Basket 4, is the basket shape spherical (more round than average) or conventional?  [22:17:37] director: Basket 4 is a conventional shallow OVAL “canoe” basket, not spherical/round. [22:18:31] matcher: We're done. Clicking submit.  [22:18:38] director: Sounds good—if anything looks off after submit, we can adjust.
